items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>As the government shutdown approaches its fourth week, WSJ’s Anna Wilde Mathews breaks down why Democrats want to extend Affordable Care Act subsidies and who would be affected by them expiring.
#wsj #thewallstreetjournal #shutdown #healthcare  #aca  #obamacare</article>

items.title: Why Trump Is Spending $20B to ‘Make Argentina Great Again’ | WSJ
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>President Trump recently announced a $20 billion deal to rescue Argentina and President Javier Milei, angering U.S. farmers and putting American taxpayers at risk. On Wednesday, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent announced that the Trump Administration was also assembling an additional $20 billion aid in a private finance facility that could act as a backstop for Argentina's debt. 

WSJ explains how the Trump administration is able to rescue Argentina and why it is propping up an ideological ally.

Chapters:
0:00 Trump’s bailout
0:24 Who is President Milei?
1:31 Milei’s spending cuts and the peso
3:32 Trump’s aid
4:53 What’s at stake?

News Explainers
Some days the high-speed news cycle can bring more questions than answers. WSJ’s news explainers break down the day's biggest stories into bite-size pieces to help you make sense of the news.

#Trump #Argentina #WSJ</article>

items.title: The NFL’s Secret Weapon in London Is This Hidden Stadium Tech | WSJ Tech Behind
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Ahead of the Denver Broncos vs. New York Jets NFL international game in London, WSJ went inside Tottenham Hotspur Stadium to see the hidden engineering that helps maximize revenue by transforming this English football pitch into a stage for world tours, a boxing ring and even an American football field.

items.title: Government Shutdown: When Will the Military Troops Get Paid?
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>As the government shutdown enters its second week, more than 1.3 million active-duty U.S. military service members are at risk of missing a paycheck, if a deal isn’t reached soon. WSJ’s Shelby Holliday explains what happens now.

#wsj #thewallstreetjournal #shutdown #usmilitary #nationalguard</article>

items.content: <article>WSJ’s Jacob Passy breaks down how the ongoing U.S. government shutdown is impacting air travel, with thousands of flights delayed due to staffing shortages.</article>

items.title: How Much Longer Can Grocery Stores Delay Trump’s Tariff Price Hikes? | WSJ
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Grocery prices are on the rise, and early evidence suggests President Trump’s tariffs aren’t helping. Pineapples from Costa Rica, fish from Greece and cheese from Italy are all subject to Trump’s tariffs, but that doesn’t mean customers are seeing the price increases–yet. 

WSJ visited the grocery store chain Stew Leonard’s and spoke with the CEO to see how tariffs are affecting a handful of popular items. 

Chapters:
0:00 Tariffs and grocery prices
0:35 Inside Stew Leonard’s 
1:08 Grocery inflation
2:11 Shrimp prices
3:02 Produce prices
3:41 Aluminum prices
4:35 What’s next?

News Explainers
Some days the high-speed news cycle can bring more questions than answers. WSJ’s news explainers break down the day's biggest stories into bite-size pieces to help you make sense of the news.

#Tariffs #Inflation #WSJ</article>
